---
title: "H&M Customer Segmentation"
format:
  revealjs:
    theme:
      - simple
      - hm-style.scss
    transition: fade
    background-transition: fade
    slide-number: true
    controls: true
    progress: true
    center: false
    width: 1600
    height: 900
    margin: 0.06
execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
  message: false
---

## Problem Definition {background-image="assets/problem-definition.png" background-size="cover" background-opacity="0.18" .problem-slide}

::: {.content-panel}
**Audience:** H&M marketing, CRM, and merchandising teams.

**Problem:** H&M has a large transaction dataset, but raw purchase records do not directly explain which customers are most valuable, how they shop, or when they are most likely to buy.

**Project focus:** Use customer segmentation to turn transaction history into actionable marketing insights.
:::

## Research Objectives {background-image="assets/research-objectives.png" background-size="cover" background-opacity="0.16" .objectives-slide}

::: {.content-panel .wide-panel}
| RO | Research Objective | Team Member |
|------------------------|------------------------|------------------------|
| RO1 | TBD | Team |
| RO2 | TBD | Team |
| RO3 | Does the sales channel (online vs. store) relate to spending patterns? | Michelle |
| RO4 | How can customers be segmented using RFM value? | Frank |
| RO5 | Do purchase timing patterns differ across customer segments? | Daisy |
:::

## Importance of the Topic {background-image="assets/importance-topic.png" background-size="cover" background-opacity="0.16" .importance-slide}

::: {.content-panel}
This project gives H&M a practical way to understand customers using real purchase data.

The findings can help the audience:

-   Target high-value customers more effectively
-   Re-engage inactive or low-frequency customers
-   Compare online and store behavior
-   Time campaigns based on segment-level purchase patterns
-   Support customer-focused decision making
:::

## Why This Topic Matters {background-image="assets/why-topic-matters.png" background-size="cover" background-opacity="0.18" .matters-slide}

::: {.content-panel}
Customer segmentation helps H&M move from broad marketing to more targeted decisions.

Instead of treating all shoppers the same, the team can identify groups such as high-value loyal customers, occasional buyers, inactive customers, and customers with specific timing or channel preferences.

This can support better promotions, retention strategies, product recommendations, and campaign timing.
:::
## Why This Topic Matters

Customer segmentation helps H&M move from broad marketing to more targeted decisions.

Instead of treating all shoppers the same, the team can identify groups such as high-value loyal customers, occasional buyers, inactive customers, and customers with specific timing or channel preferences.

This can support better promotions, retention strategies, product recommendations, and campaign timing.

## Population

-   Description of the population represented in our dataset: TBD

## Sampling / Data Collection

-   Source of the data: TBD
-   How the data was originally collected: TBD

## Data Wrangling

### From Records to Data

-   TBD

### Tidying the Data

-   TBD

### Transforming the Data

-   TBD

## RO1 {background-color="#f5f5f5"}

**Lead: \[Teammate Name\]**

-   IV's and DV's: TBD
-   Plan for data wrangling: TBD
-   Plan for data visualization: TBD

## RO2 {background-color="#f5f5f5"}

**Lead: \[Teammate Name\]**

-   IV's and DV's: TBD
-   Plan for data wrangling: TBD
-   Plan for data visualization: TBD

## RO3 {background-color="#f5f5f5"}

**Lead: Michelle Diaz**

-   IV's and DV's: TBD
-   Plan for data wrangling: TBD
-   Plan for data visualization: TBD

## RO4: RFM Value Segmentation {background-image="assets/importance-topic.png" background-size="cover" background-opacity="0.16" .importance-slide}

::: {.content-panel}

**Research Objective:** How can customers be segmented using RFM value?  
**Lead:** Frank Renn

**IV’s and DV’s:**  
IVs: Recency, Frequency, Monetary value  
DV: Customer segment / RFM value group

**Plan for data wrangling:**  
Aggregate transactions by customer ID, calculate each customer’s most recent purchase, purchase count, and total spending, then assign RFM scores or clusters.

**Plan for data visualization:**  
Use a segment bar chart, RFM summary table, and scatter plot of frequency vs. monetary value colored by segment.
:::

## Conclusion

-   Summary and next steps: TBD
